findings_details.title: Binding schema documentation omits slug field added in 82ba1f91
findings_details.body: Line 26 documents the binding as {repo, issue, branch, state_key} but commit 82ba1f91 added a slug field to the schema. Any user or agent reading this SKILL.md to understand the binding format will produce code that omits slug — breaking the idempotent-rerun backward-compat path, which relies on slug being present in new bindings. Same issue in plugins/rq/skills/start/SKILL.md line 29. Fix: update both to {repo, issue, branch, state_key, slug}.

findings_details.body: Line 29 documents the binding as {repo, issue, branch, state_key} but commit 82ba1f91 added slug to the schema. An agent reading this SKILL.md to understand the binding format will produce code that omits slug. Fix: update to {repo, issue, branch, state_key, slug}.

findings_details.title: _check_idempotent_rerun: get_owner_repo() may return spurious owner/repo for file:// remotes causing plan-path divergence from original run
findings_details.body: get_owner_repo() extracts the last two path components of the remote URL. For a file:// remote like file:///tmp/bare-repo.git it returns 'tmp/bare-repo' which passes the ^[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+/[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+$ regex, so plan_repo='tmp/bare-repo' and _rehydrate_quality_state looks for the plan in ~/.claude/plans/bare-repo/. But the original /rq:start run called 'gh repo view' (rq-start.sh line 128) which fails for no-GH repos, so original plan_repo=basename(repo_root). If repo_root basename != 'bare-repo', the idempotent rerun cannot find the plan file and rescaffolds it. Impact: narrow but real — affects CI test repos and local bare repos whose file:// path basename differs from the checkout basename. Fix: store plan_repo in branch-issue.json alongside slug so the idempotent path is fully deterministic. Alternatively, validate that get_owner_repo() result does not start with a known filesystem path component (tmp, var, home) before accepting it.

findings_details.title: test_rq_start.sh: REPO_WITH_MAIN uses 'git init -q' without -b main, causing --new argument-validation tests to fail on systems where git default is 'master'
findings_details.body: Line 91-96 creates REPO_WITH_MAIN with 'git init -q' (no -b flag). On git >= 2.28 with init.defaultBranch=master (the git-distributed default), the repo lands on 'master'. rq-start.sh checks 'cur != default_branch' before processing --new arguments, so the script exits 1 with 'must start from main (currently on master)' before reaching handle_new_issue(). Three tests that assert usage/validation behavior (exit 2, slug error) instead see exit 1. Fix: change line 93 to 'git init -q -b main' to match what the tests expect. Same fix pattern already used at line 73 ('git init -q -b main PRIMARY').
